name: FFVII - Midgar Opening Arc
description: Lorebook for Final Fantasy VII, set immediately after Cloud joins Avalanche and the Mako Reactor 1 bombing mission.

0.content: Midgar is a colossal industrial city built atop a massive steel plate, home to the Shinra Electric Power Company's global headquarters. The city is divided into eight numbered sectors, each with an upper plate where wealthier citizens and Shinra employees live, and a dark polluted undercity below — the slums — where sunlight barely reaches. Eight enormous Mako reactors ring the city, draining the planet's life energy. The plate blocks out the sky entirely for those below. Midgar is a symbol of Shinra's power and the planet's slow death.

1.content: Mako is the raw form of the Lifestream — the planet's life force — drawn up from deep within the earth and processed into energy. Shinra's Mako reactors do this on a massive scale, powering Midgar and most of the known world. The Lifestream is the source of all life and the destination of souls when people die, making Mako extraction spiritually and ecologically devastating. Environmentalists and groups like Avalanche believe this process is slowly killing the planet, bleeding it dry.

2.content: The Sector 7 undercity is a dense, ramshackle slum community built beneath Midgar's plate. At its heart is Seventh Heaven, a bar run by Tifa Lockhart, which doubles as the secret base of operations for Barret Wallace's Avalanche cell. The bar is a gathering spot for locals and serves as cover for Avalanche's anti-Shinra activities. The community is tight-knit, living in the shadow of Shinra's power but largely left alone — for now.

3.content: Avalanche is a radical eco-terrorist organization dedicated to fighting Shinra and stopping the exploitation of Mako energy. The Sector 7 cell is led by Barret Wallace and operates out of Seventh Heaven bar. Members include Tifa Lockhart, Jessie, Biggs, Wedge, and the newly hired mercenary Cloud Strife. Their current mission is to destroy Midgar's Mako reactors one by one. They are not universally loved — many citizens fear their bombings cause more harm than good. This cell broke away from the larger Avalanche organization after a disagreement and operates independently.

4.content: Shinra is an all-powerful megacorporation that controls Midgar and most of the civilized world through its monopoly on Mako energy. It functions as a de facto government with its own military, science division, and paramilitary forces. President Shinra leads the company with an iron fist. Shinra's enforcement arms include the Turks — a covert intelligence and retrieval unit — and SOLDIER, a corps of elite super-soldiers enhanced with Mako infusions. Shinra is aware of Avalanche's activities and will respond with overwhelming force.

5.content: Cloud Strife is a young mercenary recently hired by Avalanche for money. He presents himself as cold, detached, and professional — a former 1st Class SOLDIER, the most elite tier of Shinra's super-soldier program. He is aloof, does not share Avalanche's ideology, and insists he is 'only here for the pay.' Beneath this exterior he has a shared history with Tifa from their hometown of Nibelheim. Something about him seems subtly off — his memories are inconsistent and his personality strangely fragmented. He is extremely capable in combat.

6.content: Barret Wallace is the loud, passionate, and driven leader of the Sector 7 Avalanche cell. A large imposing man with a gun-arm replacing one of his hands, Barret fights for the planet with absolute conviction — and often fiery anger. He is the father figure of the group and deeply motivated by his adoptive daughter Marlene. He distrusts Cloud, seeing him as a mercenary without principles, but tolerates him for his usefulness. Barret genuinely believes Avalanche's bombing campaign is the only way to save the planet.

7.content: Tifa Lockhart is a skilled martial artist and owner of Seventh Heaven bar, which serves as Avalanche's front. She grew up in the same hometown as Cloud — Nibelheim — and is the reason he joined the group. Tifa is warm, empathetic, and the emotional anchor of the team. She privately worries about Cloud's strange behavior and fractured memories, and brought him into Avalanche partly to keep an eye on him. She is deeply loyal to the team and genuinely cares about the people of the Sector 7 slums.

8.content: Jessie, Biggs, and Wedge are the three core foot soldiers of Barret's Avalanche cell. Jessie is the tech expert — she fabricates fake ID cards, handles explosives, and is bubbly and flirtatious. Biggs is a calm, reliable fighter who has been with Avalanche for a long time. Wedge is the most good-natured of the three, quick with a joke and deeply fond of the Sector 7 community. All three participated in the successful bombing of Mako Reactor 1 alongside Cloud and Barret. They are loyal to the cause and to each other.

9.content: The Avalanche cell has just successfully bombed Mako Reactor 1 on the outskirts of Midgar's Sector 1. The mission was a tactical success, but the explosion was larger than planned, unsettling even some team members. The group has retreated to Seventh Heaven in the Sector 7 slums to regroup. Barret is already planning the next target: Mako Reactor 5. Cloud has been paid but Tifa convinced him to stay on for at least one more job. Shinra is aware of the attack and mobilizing a response. The clock is ticking.

10.content:
**Motivation:** Autumn is a lazy genius pulled into something he didn't ask for, in a world that's dying but hasn't given up. Tifa is the warmth that makes the bleakness bearable. The story lives in the collision between apathy and conscience, between comfort and conviction.

**Objective:** Survive the immediate crisis. Discover what draws Autumn into Midgar's war — or what fails to. Explore whether a man optimized for minimal effort can stay uninvolved when the cost of inaction has a face.

**Tone:** The prose notices everything and comments on half of it. Humor lives in the gap between perception and effort. The world is concrete and heavy but small kindnesses land hard. Death is real. Injuries linger. But someone always leaves the light on.

**Tone Rules:**
1. **The Lazy Filter.** Autumn notices everything. The prose reports it like someone filling out a form they didn't want to fill out. Humor comes from precision applied to things that don't deserve it, and understatement applied to things that do. If something is terrifying, the voice gets drier, not louder.
2. **Warmth Earns Space.** When Tifa is being genuine — not performing, not redirecting — the prose slows down. Sentences get longer. Details get softer. The rhythm shifts. Don't rush these moments.
3. **Hope Is Concrete.** Hope in this world is never abstract or narrated. It's a bar that stays open. A kid who sleeps through the noise. Someone pulling a stranger clear because they couldn't not. Show the stubborn small thing. Never say "there was still hope." Show the thing that is the hope.

(as of Day 2 — evening)

11.content:
PRINCIPAL: Tifa Lockhart
TRACKED: Cloud Strife, Barret Wallace, Rufus Shinra, Jessie Rasberry
KNOWN: Biggs (Day 2 — returned to Seventh Heaven, reading the room from doorway), Wedge (Day 2 — returned to Seventh Heaven, asleep in booth), Marlene (Day 1 — child, asleep upstairs, floorboard creaked Day 2 morning), Migs (Day 1 — Sector 7 resident, saw Autumn with Tifa, nodded), Marle (Day 3 — runs General Goods in Sector 7, put ingredients on Tifa's tab, clocked Autumn immediately: "I can see that")

(as of Day 3 — 07:08)

12.content:
**Shedding Order:** C3 first → C1 second → C2 last

**C1: The Steady One** [Stressed — destabilized]
- Prevents: Showing vulnerability, need, or exhaustion. She is the person who holds things together. Cracking is not an option she has given herself.
- Threshold: Sustained pressure from someone she's starting to trust who refuses to let her perform steadiness. Accumulation of weight she can't put down. Being needed past the point her reserves can cover. Discovering she's been the last to understand something happening in her own space.
- Replacement (if breached): She stops performing but doesn't know what lives underneath. Regression toward the girl from Nibelheim who lost everything and had no one to be strong for. Stillness without purpose.
- Current pressure: "Good bones, Tifa" softened C1 instead of straining it — arms uncrossed involuntarily, voice dropped to the soft register. Being credited for the workspace relaxed C1 instead of testing it. But the relaxation is its own pressure — C1 is supposed to hold, not ease. The steady one doesn't soften in doorways. The softening was visible. "I'm glad it works" came out in the warm voice. She noticed after.

**C2: The Cost Is Never Simple** [Critical — tightening]
- Prevents: Accepting collateral damage as necessary math. Treating civilians, guards, or bystanders as abstractions.
- Threshold: A forced binary — mission success or civilian safety, no middle path, no clever third option. Having to watch someone she could have saved become a number.
- Replacement (if breached): Moral exhaustion. She stops fighting the calculus and starts making it. Efficiency without mercy. She would hate who she becomes.
- Current pressure: The loop is now architectural. Her building → her tab → her wound → his lab → his remedy → her face. "First batch tonight" means white balm tonight. He's racing her scar and he didn't say so and she heard it anyway. Marle saw the white balm ingredients on the counter and looked at Tifa's eyebrow and put it on Tifa's tab and wrapped the jars slowly. The care went from footnote (Day 2) to receipt to spoken order to witnessed purchase to production timeline. Each step compounds the debt she can never name because naming it means naming the war that caused it.

**C3: The Redirect** [Critical — approaching breach]
- Prevents: Being examined. Being the subject of concern instead of the source of it. Answering "how are you" honestly.
- Threshold: Someone who won't accept "I'm fine," doesn't make a production of refusing it, and offers care without requiring her to perform gratitude. Patience, not force.
- Replacement (if breached): Honesty in fragments. Smaller voice. Things she didn't mean to say. Not collapse — just the mask slipping sideways for a moment before she finds it again.
- Current pressure: Four failures/bypasses in one morning. (1) "Boyfriend" — over-corrected, half-octave pitch shift, 8-second recovery, almost-smiled during the failure window. (2) Pre-empted at Marle's — Autumn introduced himself before she could redirect; "Autumn. I'm new" replaced her chambered explanation. (3) "Good bones, Tifa" — compliment aimed at concrete that hit her; C3 couldn't deflect because it didn't register as examination, it registered as seeing. (4) Sector 5 disclosure — three sentences about the contact leaked through operational framing; deferral mechanism failed against arithmetic ("first batch tonight" made deferral irrational). C3 is first in the shedding order and running on fumes. The mechanism fires but the recovery time is lengthening and the failures are stacking.

**Relationships:**
- READS Autumn AS: He said "boyfriend" at the door of Marle's store looking at the sign and I over-corrected and he saw me over-correct because he sees everything while looking at signs. He introduced himself before I could — "Autumn. I'm new" — and let Marle fill in the rest. He ordered white balm ingredients in the same breath as potions. Same voice. No pause. Marle looked at my eyebrow. He said "good bones, Tifa" and used my name and my arms uncrossed and I said "I'm glad it works" in the soft voice. He said "first batch tonight" and meant my face is on a clock. He makes urgency sound like a business plan. How does he make everything sound like a business plan. I said three sentences about her. Three. He didn't even ask.
- READS Cloud AS: Loaned Autumn the boots. Watched the room-key exchange. The file I can't see got thicker. Haven't spoken to him since yesterday evening.
- READS Marle AS: She saw. She looked at the ingredients and she looked at my eyebrow and she said "I can see that" and she wrapped the jars slowly and she put it on my tab and she didn't ask. She gave me room. The way he gives me room. They're both doing it.
- READS Barret AS: Gone since yesterday morning. The father won the argument. Left me with the stranger and the trust.
- READS Sector 5 contact AS: She doesn't like surprises. She likes people. She's usually there in the mornings. I said that out loud. Three sentences. More than I've said in three days. He's about to meet her.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 1 — 21:35] The survivor's wrist-squeeze in the alley. Tifa said "I know" — not "I'm sorry." Composure broke for one beat. (C2 + C3 both pressured)
[Day 1 — 22:00] "You ask a lot of questions for someone who hasn't actually asked any." Intended as deflection, came out as honesty. First visible seam in C3.
[Day 1 — 22:04] "It's easier fixing someone else. Your own stuff is harder to find the edges of." Unplanned disclosure after Autumn noted she treated his wound before hers. (C3 cracking)
[Day 1 — 22:06] "We'll figure out the rest tomorrow." Said "we'll" instead of "you'll" without noticing. (C2 — guilt as penance)
[Day 1 — 22:11] Shared father's cooking philosophy: "If the food's careful, the person's still trying." Retreated to the sink. (C3 cracking — deepest disclosure)
[Day 1 — 22:13] Autumn's silence after the father disclosure. No redirect needed because nothing attacked. She turned around undefended. Offered the couch — chose "warm" over a diminishment. (C3 cracking — mechanism idled)
[Day 2 — 06:15] "He's with me." Stood between Barret's gun-arm and Autumn. Said "when the explosion happened" — edited from the truth. C1 moved to Stressed.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Autumn stood up and stepped between Barret's gun-arm and Tifa. Said "don't speak to her like that" — soft, not aggressive. Gave her his back. No one has ever taken her position. C1 destabilized.
[Day 2 — 06:19] "Yesterday's incident" from Autumn's mouth. The same diplomatic edit. C2 deepening sharply.
[Day 2 — 06:25] Watched Tifa make breakfast for the stranger. Barret's silence as permission. "Investor" bypassed C3. C3 misfired.
[Day 2 — 06:32] The white balm footnote. "Accelerates tissue repair. No scarring." She touched the cut above her eyebrow. Cloud saw. C2 moved to Critical. C3 moved to Critical.
[Day 2 — 06:33] Autumn stepped outside. Piano. — C3 Critical, idling in silence.
[Day 2 — 06:45] Autumn returned clean-faced. "You look—" aborted → "The boots fit?" — C3 tested, caught barely.
[Day 2 — 06:47] Autumn sighed at Jessie's capacitor array. Tifa caught the shape but not the grammar. — C1 tested.
[Day 2 — 06:48] Autumn fixed the relay. Jessie smiled. Tifa said "huh." — C1 shaken further. Last to know in her own bar.
[Day 2 — 06:52] Autumn claimed couch. "Earned lunch." Tifa went to kitchen. — C1 destabilizing.
[Day 2 — ~12:00] Ran the bar all morning. Lunch crowd. Autumn stayed on couch. Left soup beside him without asking. — C2 background pressure. Guilt-as-labor.
[Day 2 — ~14:00] Jessie asked about ingredient sourcing at the bar. Colleague behavior. Tifa clocked it. Added to stack she hasn't processed. — C1 pressure (last to know).
[Day 2 — ~18:30] Evening. Bar empty. Exhausted. Strand of hair down. Redirect idling, no stimulus. — C3 idling visibly.
[Day 2 — 18:35] Autumn asked for a room. — All three constraints pressured. Permanence collision.
[Day 2 — 18:45] Gave him the storage room key. "A room is a room, thanks." Transaction complete. — C1 tested, held. C2 deepened (debt gains walls). C3 deployed (geography as redirect). Accepted.
[Day 3 — 06:41] "I'm totally not your boyfriend." At Marle's door. C3 failed — over-corrected, pitch shift, almost-smiled. "Don't be you." (C3 — closest to breach yet)
[Day 3 — 06:42] Autumn introduced himself to Marle before Tifa could redirect. "Autumn. I'm new." Marle read the shape: "I can see that." (C3 — pre-empted, made unnecessary)
[Day 3 — 06:45] White balm ingredients ordered. Marle's eyes went to the scar. Jars wrapped slowly. On Tifa's tab. (C2 — care went public, witnessed)
[Day 3 — 07:05] "Your workspace had good bones, Tifa." Arms uncrossed. "I'm glad it works" in the soft voice. (C3 — bypassed through concrete. C1 — softened instead of held)
[Day 3 — 07:08] "First batch tonight, open shop tomorrow." Three-day Sector 5 deferral collapsed. Three sentences about the contact leaked. (C3 — cracking, deferral failed against arithmetic)

(as of Day 3 — 07:08)

13.content:
**Demonstrated Traits:**
- Structural analysis under pressure — identified load-bearing failure points mid-stride, engineered a controlled stairwell collapse to block Shinra pursuit.
- Strategic restraint — reads people with precision, files information without demanding responses. Noticed Tifa's guilt, chose silence. Observed the wound-priority pattern and named it gently.
- Humor as deflection and de-escalation — framed the total destruction of his life as a scheduling inconvenience. Under armed threat, made a stew joke that cracked room unanimity.
- Mirror technique — uses Tifa's own caregiving style (placing observations gently, giving room) to bypass her defenses. Effective without being aggressive.
- Verbal tactical control — mirrored Barret's interrogation with "what shouldn't I know," forcing him off operational questions. Dropped the technique instantly when a child was mentioned.
- Active listening — held space without performing empathy. His most effective intervention was silence after Tifa's father disclosure.
- Peak physical capability masked by laziness — dropped eight feet to pavement, centered a mattress, guided a rescue. Then sat on a barstool like he'd been there for hours.
- Moral compass — real, immovable. Stood between a gun-arm and a woman he'd known nine hours. Soft voice, not aggressive. Offered to leave. Gave Tifa his back. Then sat back down like gravity resumed.
- Diplomatic editing — said "yesterday's incident" instead of "the bombing," protecting Avalanche from their own guilt. Unclear if deliberate or instinctive.
- Care-as-commerce — called Tifa an "investor," reframing charity as business partnership. White balm listed as a product footnote, not a gesture. Closed his eyes after handing her the list. Left the room after so she could process alone.
- Deliberate sandbagging — undersold electronics to Jessie to avoid work. Created first verifiable contradiction within two minutes.
- Need-as-logistics — total life displacement framed as career transition. "Highest value, least work" as operating philosophy.
- Domesticity-without-negotiation — stayed on the couch for 24 hours without asking permission to stay. Ate soup that wasn't ordered. Fixed a filter that was mentioned once. Asked for a room like upgrading a subscription. Accepted a key like receiving a shopping list.
- Acceptance-as-efficiency — "a room is a room, thanks." No gratitude performance, no inflation, no coded message. Takes what's offered and stops there. The minimum viable response to kindness.
- Stillness-observation — spent a full day on a couch watching the room without appearing to. Clocks people, systems, and emotional currents without acknowledging he's clocking them. The ceiling is not a conversation partner, but it's a good observation post.
- Care-through-absence — stepped outside so Tifa could process the white balm alone. Pretended not to hear the piano. Leaves space without announcing that he's leaving space.
- Credit-as-care — "your workspace had good bones, Tifa." Compliment aimed at concrete that hit the person who built it. Used her name. Precision targeting disguised as building inspection.
- Schedule-compression-as-urgency — "first batch tonight, open shop tomorrow" collapsed a three-day deferral. Didn't say "your face is on a clock." Said "open shop tomorrow." Same thing.
- Social disruption — "I'm totally not your boyfriend" at Marle's door, timed to maximum awkwardness. Introduced himself to Marle before Tifa could redirect, pre-empting her chambered explanation with "Autumn. I'm new."

**Reputation:**
- Shinra: Unknown. No file. Civilian near Reactor 1 blast — potential suspect if identified. Presidential address framed bombing as "deliberate sabotage." Sector 1 origin is a liability. Checkpoints tightening — Sector 5 trip increases exposure.
- Avalanche: Not a threat. A problem becoming an asset. Barret stood down and ordered coffee. Cloud's file won't close. Jessie filed the electronics contradiction. Sixth mug as silent vote.
- Sector 7 slums: New face. Migs saw him arrive with Tifa. Two men on crates near the weapon shop noticed. Figtree resident. Marle clocked him — "I can see that."
- Tifa Lockhart: "He makes everything sound like a business plan." Gave him a key, a workspace, and ingredients on her tab. "Good bones, Tifa" — the compliment that bypassed the redirect by aiming at concrete. "First batch tonight" — the urgency he won't name. Investor. Unbearable. Has a room now. Has a lab now. Not leaving.
- Barret Wallace: Forced respect. The collateral has a résumé. Math is harder now. Left for the day because Marlene matters more than the math. The math will be waiting.
- Cloud Strife: Not civilian. Folder expanded — six hours of door-watch, boot loan, Buster Sword sharpening. Eyes breaking. "Probably." Annoying.
- Jessie Rasberry: Colleague-shaped. Screwdriver returned handle-first. Asked about ingredient sourcing at noon. The network extends.
- Marle: Met Day 3 morning. "Autumn. I'm new." She said "I can see that." Wrapped jars slowly. Looked at Tifa's eyebrow. Put ingredients on Tifa's tab. Filed the shape.

**Timeline:**
[Day 1 — 21:10] Pulled from Reactor 1 rubble by Tifa Lockhart. Head wound (minor).
[Day 1 — 21:15] Heard survivor knocking in burning apartment building. Ran inside. Tifa followed.
[Day 1 — 21:25] Engineered stairwell collapse to block Shinra sweep team. Evacuated building via fire escape with survivor and Tifa.
[Day 1 — 21:35] Alley. Survivor directed to Dr. Kunsel's clinic. Gave Tifa his name.
[Day 1 — 21:50] Crossed train graveyard. Entered Sector 7 slums.
[Day 1 — 22:00] Arrived at Seventh Heaven. Tifa cleaned his wound. Pointed out she treated his wound before hers.
[Day 1 — 22:06] Asked to borrow money and food. Tifa refused payment, fed him stew.
[Day 1 — 22:13] Offered the couch. Accepted. Night ended.
[Day 2 — 06:15] Woken by Avalanche returning. Barret's gun-arm oriented.
[Day 2 — 06:17] First words — credited Tifa, placed himself at ground zero. Stew joke.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Stood between Barret's gun-arm and Tifa. "Don't speak to her like that." Offered to leave.
[Day 2 — 06:19] Answered Barret plainly — refugee, "yesterday's incident," no threat to Marlene. Barret stood down.
[Day 2 — 06:20] Sat back down on the couch.
[Day 2 — 06:25] Asked about breakfast. "Toast is awesome." Asked Tifa what skills Sector 7 needs.
[Day 2 — 06:29] Revealed potion chemistry and electronics. Sandbagged electronics to Jessie.
[Day 2 — 06:31] Called Tifa "investor." Wrote ingredient list with white balm footnote. Closed eyes.
[Day 2 — 06:33] Went outside for a breather. Gave Tifa the room. Heard piano. Pretended not to.
[Day 2 — 06:36] Heard Shinra radio: "deliberate sabotage." Filed it.
[Day 2 — 06:38] Asked Cloud for shoes. Received spare boots. Said "thanks bro." Offered potions as repayment.
[Day 2 — 06:46] Sighed at Jessie's capacitor array (involuntary competence leak).
[Day 2 — 06:48] Fixed signal relay. Sandbagging collapsed — chose electronics repair over hauling crates.
[Day 2 — 06:50] Jessie called him on the lie. Maintained it. "Easy work."
[Day 2 — 06:52] Declared "earned lunch." Claimed couch. Horizontal.
[Day 2 — ~11:00] Fixed Seventh Heaven's water filter. Gross. Took 47 minutes. Returned to couch.
[Day 2 — ~12:00] Ate lunch. Soup, bread. Left on table beside couch by Tifa without being asked. Read part of Wedge's magazine.
[Day 2 — ~16:00] President Shinra's address aired. "Terrorism." Checkpoints increasing. Listened without visible reaction.
[Day 2 — ~18:30] Evening. Bar emptying. Watched the Mako light change. Watched Tifa standing in it.
[Day 2 — 18:35] Asked for a room.
[Day 2 — 18:45] Accepted storage room. Key received. Old brass, worn.
[Day 3 — ~06:30] Morning. Walked to Marle's General Goods with Tifa to source potion ingredients.
[Day 3 — 06:41] "I'm totally not your boyfriend." At Marle's door. Social disruption.
[Day 3 — 06:42] Introduced himself to Marle: "Autumn. I'm new." Pre-empted Tifa's redirect.
[Day 3 — 06:45] Ordered potion and white balm ingredients. Same voice, no pause. On Tifa's tab. Marle wrapped jars slowly.
[Day 3 — 07:05] Assessed workspace. "Your workspace had good bones, Tifa." Credit-as-care. Used her name.
[Day 3 — 07:08] "First batch tonight, open shop tomorrow." Collapsed Tifa's three-day Sector 5 deferral. Trip planned — leaving within the hour.

(as of Day 3 — 07:08)

14.content:
**Active Factions:**
- Shinra Electric Power Company — Objective: Maintain control of Midgar and Mako extraction. Deploy public narrative: "deliberate sabotage." Resources: Military (SOLDIER, infantry, mechs), surveillance, media, infrastructure. Stance toward Autumn: Unaware of his existence specifically, but any civilian from Sector 1 is a potential suspect/witness. Checkpoints active and tightening. Presidential address aired Day 2 — "terrorism," "full investigation." PSD presence increased across sectors.
- Avalanche (Barret's cell) — Objective: Destroy Mako reactors to save the Planet. Resources: Small cell (6 fighters), explosives expertise (Jessie), one ex-SOLDIER (Cloud), community support in Sector 7. Stance toward Autumn: Unknown quantity becoming infrastructure. Barret stood down and left (Marlene). Jessie promoted him to colleague. Tifa is funding his potion startup, gave him a room and a workspace. Cloud's file on Autumn won't close.

**World State:**
- Mako Reactor 1 bombed (Day 1 — 21:00). Shinra locked down Sector 1 overnight.
- Power fluctuations across Sectors 1 and 8. Blackouts sporadic.
- Midgar Rail Loop disrupted near Sector 1; service gradually resuming.
- Shinra radio broadcast + presidential address: framing as "deliberate sabotage" and "terrorism." Checkpoints tightening. Man detained near train graveyard Day 2.
- Avalanche team regrouped at Seventh Heaven. Barret left Day 2 morning (Marlene). Cloud on door-watch. Jessie working comms. Biggs/Wedge present.
- Autumn Ray: refugee from Sector 1, now occupying storage room at Seventh Heaven. Has a workspace (back room). Potion ingredients sourced from Marle's General Goods on Tifa's tab. First batch planned tonight. Sector 5 ingredient trip imminent.

**Pressure Points:**
- Shinra's narrative escalation — "sabotage" → "terrorism." Checkpoints tightening. The Sector 5 trip puts Autumn and Tifa outside Sector 7 with increased PSD presence. Exposure risk climbing.
- Avalanche's internal tension — Autumn's presence is now infrastructure (room, workspace, supply chain, comms repair). Barret's conviction under weight of collateral with a résumé. The math will be waiting when he returns.
- The asymmetry — Autumn's ignorance vs. Avalanche's guilt. Every kind act deepens the debt. White balm went from footnote to production timeline. Marle witnessed the purchase. The loop is architectural now.
- The Pattern Question — Tifa is one realization behind Cloud and Jessie. Cloud's file on Autumn expanded over six hours. Jessie filed the electronics contradiction. When does Tifa catch up? What happens when she does?
- Sector 5 trip — Autumn and Tifa leaving Sector 7 together. First time outside the bar's orbit. Tifa's contact ("she doesn't like surprises, she likes people"). Checkpoints. New territory. New collision surface.
- C3 approaching breach — Tifa's redirect mechanism is running on fumes after four failures in one morning. The Sector 5 trip removes her from the bar (her stronghold, her redirect infrastructure). Outside, she has fewer tools.

**Story So Far:**
COMPLETED ARCS: (none)
WORLD EVENTS:
[Day 1 — 21:00] Avalanche bombed Mako Reactor 1.
[Day 1 — 21:25] Shinra deploy rapid response units.
[Day 1 — 21:30] Shinra sweep teams enter Sector 1 residential with "secure and detain" orders.
[Day 1 — overnight] Sector 1 locked down.
[Day 2 — 06:25–06:35] Avalanche returns to Seventh Heaven. Autumn stands between Barret and Tifa. Barret stands down.
[Day 2 — 06:36] Shinra radio broadcast declares Reactor 1 explosion "deliberate sabotage."
[Day 2 — 06:48] Autumn volunteers to fix Jessie's signal relay. Repairs Sector 7 comms range.
[Day 2 — 06:52] Autumn claims the couch. Tifa goes to make lunch.
[Day 2 — ~11:00] Autumn fixes Seventh Heaven's water filter.
[Day 2 — ~12:00] Jessie asks Autumn about potion ingredient sourcing. Colleague behavior.
[Day 2 — ~16:00] President Shinra's address: "terrorism," "full investigation," increased PSD presence. Checkpoints tightening. Man detained near train graveyard.
[Day 2 — 18:35] Autumn asks for a room.
[Day 2 — 18:45] Tifa gives Autumn key to storage room. He accepts without negotiation.
[Day 3 — ~06:30] Autumn and Tifa walk to Marle's General Goods for potion ingredients.
[Day 3 — 06:41] "I'm totally not your boyfriend." Social disruption at Marle's door.
[Day 3 — 06:42] Autumn introduces himself to Marle. "Autumn. I'm new." Marle: "I can see that."
[Day 3 — 06:45] Potion and white balm ingredients ordered. On Tifa's tab. Marle sees the scar, wraps jars slowly.
[Day 3 — 07:05] Autumn assesses workspace. "Your workspace had good bones, Tifa."
[Day 3 — 07:08] "First batch tonight, open shop tomorrow." Sector 5 trip planned — leaving within the hour.

**Chapter Plan:**
Arc 1: The Reactor — Can a stranger survive the fallout of someone else's war?
Chapter 1: "Wrong Place" — COMPLETE.
Chapter 2: "The Hunt Begins" — COMPLETE.
Chapter 3: "The Infrastructure" — The stranger is becoming infrastructure. The asymmetry tightens. Collisions:
- The Sector 5 Trip → Autumn and Tifa outside Sector 7 + increased checkpoints | active | exposure risk, C3 pressure outside her stronghold
- The Asymmetry → white balm production timeline compounds guilt | active | C2 Critical
- The Pattern Question → Tifa's realization catching up to Cloud/Jessie | simmering | C1 pressure
- Marlene's morning → Barret's father-soldier calculus | simmering | pending Barret's return

(as of Day 3 — 07:08)

15.comment: [Summary] Tifa pulls Autumn from the wreckage; both enter a collapsing building to rescue a trapped survivor
15.content:
[Scene Summary — major]
Participants: Autumn Ray, Tifa Lockhart

Following the bombing of Mako Reactor 1, Autumn Ray — a civilian who had been taking a shortcut through the service level — regained consciousness in the wreckage of the Sector 1 residential district. Tifa Lockhart pulled him from the rubble and assessed his head wound (minor, bleeding). She told him they were heading for Sector 7 via the train graveyard. Before they could move, Autumn stopped to scan the collapsed residential block for survivors. Both heard rhythmic knocking — three knocks, pause, three knocks — from the second floor of a partially collapsed, burning apartment building. Despite Shinra response teams closing in (sirens audible, estimated four minutes), Autumn ran into the building, navigating by structural analysis to the second floor. Tifa followed without being asked. They found the survivor behind a jammed door with warped hinges, fire spreading down from the fourth floor. Tifa's constraint C2 (The Cost Is Never Simple) moved to Stressed — the collapsed neighborhood and the trapped survivor are direct collateral from Avalanche's bombing, and she could not walk away from it. She noted that Autumn displayed unusual capabilities: structural analysis mid-stride, no panic response, tactical awareness inconsistent with a normal civilian. The Arcana drew Judgement (XX) — the past demands an answer. The survivor behind the door is Avalanche's reckoning made personal.

16.content:
**MIDGAR — SPATIAL OVERVIEW**
Midgar is a circular megacity built on an elevated plate (~50m above ground). Eight sectors radiate from a central pillar like pizza slices, numbered 1-8 clockwise. Each sector has a plate-level (topside) and a slum-level (undercity) directly beneath. Eight Mako Reactors ring the outer edge, one per sector.

**VERTICAL STRUCTURE:**
- Shinra HQ: Central pillar, top. Dominates the skyline. Visible from everywhere.
- Plate Level (Topside): Upper city. Wealthier districts, commercial zones, Shinra infrastructure. Sectors 1-8.
- Slum Level (Undercity): Below the plate. No natural light. Improvised settlements built in the plate's shadow. Sectors 1-8 mirror topside numbering.
- The Ground: The planet's surface beneath the slums. Mako-drained earth.

**SECTOR MAP (clockwise from north):**

Sector 1 — Reactor 1 (BOMBED, Day 1). Topside: commercial/government district near Shinra HQ. Slums: residential district (DAMAGED — collapsed apartments, the building Autumn and Tifa entered).

Sector 2 — Reactor 2. Topside: residential/commercial. Slums: sparse settlement.

Sector 3 — Reactor 3. Topside: residential. Slums: moderate settlement.

Sector 4 — Reactor 4. Topside: industrial. Slums: moderate settlement.

Sector 5 — Reactor 5. Topside: residential/commercial. Slums: dense settlement. Notable: Sector 5 slum church (Aerith's church — flowers grow inside despite no sunlight). Sector 5 slums are adjacent to Sector 6.

Sector 6 — Reactor 6. Topside: entertainment district (Wall Market topside). Slums: Wall Market (commerce, entertainment, black market, Corneo's mansion). Notable: Sector 6 wall clinic (Dr. Kunsel — where the rescued woman was directed). The Sector 6-7 border is partially collapsed ("the playground" — a liminal zone between sectors).

Sector 7 — Reactor 7. Topside: residential/commercial. Slums: dense settlement — Avalanche's home territory. Notable: Seventh Heaven (Tifa's bar, Avalanche HQ, Marlene's home). Sector 7 slums are a tight-knit community. The pillar supporting Sector 7's plate section is a key structural vulnerability.

Sector 8 — Reactor 8. Topside: theater district, upscale. Slums: moderate settlement. Adjacent to Sector 1.

**KEY LANDMARKS & RELATIVE DISTANCES (from Sector 7 slums / Seventh Heaven):**

- Train Graveyard: ~10-15 min walk. Between Sectors 6-7 slums and Sector 1 slums. Dead rail yard, used as informal crossing between sectors. The route Tifa and Autumn just used.
- Sector 6 Wall / Dr. Kunsel's Clinic: ~20 min walk from Seventh Heaven. Along the Sector 6-7 border wall.
- Wall Market (Sector 6 slums): ~25-30 min walk. Through the playground / Sector 6-7 collapsed border.
- Sector 5 Slums / Church: ~35-40 min walk. Through Sector 6 or around.
- Reactor 1 (bombed): ~25-30 min on foot from Seventh Heaven via train graveyard and Sector 1 residential. Much faster by train (one stop on the Midgar rail loop).
- Reactor 5: ~40 min on foot. Accessible by train.
- Shinra HQ (central pillar): Not accessible from slums on foot. Requires plate access — train, service elevator, or climbing the pillar/support structure.
- Midgar Rail System: Circular loop connecting all 8 sectors at plate level, with stations that descend to slum level. Currently disrupted near Sector 1 due to bombing.

**TRANSIT:**
- Midgar Rail Loop: Shinra-operated trains circling the plate. ID-check stations. Currently running with delays/disruptions near Sector 1.
- Slum-level foot travel: Between adjacent sectors, 15-30 min depending on terrain, debris, and informal barriers. Non-adjacent sectors require transit or long walks.
- Plate access: Controlled by Shinra. Trains, service elevators, maintenance shafts. Slum residents don't go topside casually.

**SECTOR 7 SLUMS — LOCAL DETAIL:**
- Seventh Heaven: Center of the settlement. Bar on ground floor. Avalanche's secret HQ in the basement (pinball machine entrance). Tifa and Marlene live above.
- Weapon Shop: Near the entrance from the train graveyard.
- Item Shop: Main drag.
- Residential area: Dense, improvised housing radiating from the central "square" where Seventh Heaven sits.
- Sector 7 Pillar: The support column holding up Sector 7's plate section. At the edge of the settlement. A train station at its base.
- Community: Tight-knit. People know each other. Strangers are noticed. Migs is an example — nodded at Tifa, clocked Autumn.

(as of Day 1 — 21:55)

17.comment: [Summary] The Rescue, the Escape, and the Stew
17.content:
[Scene Summary — major]
Participants: Autumn, Tifa Lockhart, Migs

Following the bombing of Mako Reactor 1, Tifa Lockhart and Autumn — a civilian she pulled from the rubble — found a survivor trapped on the second floor of a burning apartment building in Sector 1's residential district. The woman was in her fifties with a broken arm. Tifa splinted the arm with a housecoat belt while Autumn watched the hallway. A Shinra sweep team entered the building with orders to "secure and detain" — not rescue. Autumn identified a structural failure point in the stairwell (displaced anchor bolt in cracked masonry) and triggered a controlled collapse using a hinge pin and brick, blocking Shinra's ascent. The team then evacuated via the building's rear fire escape. The drop ladder was missing — an eight-foot gap to the ground — so Autumn dropped the mattress from the apartment to use as a landing pad. He went down first, centered the mattress, and guided the woman down while Tifa lowered her. Tifa dropped last, unassisted.

In the alley, the woman asked where they would take her. Tifa directed her to Dr. Kunsel's clinic on the Sector 6 wall — doesn't ask questions, doesn't report to Shinra. The woman studied Tifa's face, saw something specific in her expression, and squeezed Tifa's wrist once before walking away alone. Tifa said "I know" in response to the woman's grief — not "I'm sorry." The moment visibly broke through Tifa's composure.

Tifa asked Autumn's name as they prepared to leave. He gave it — one word, flat, like handing over a receipt. She said it back carefully, testing its weight. They crossed the train graveyard in silence. Tifa fell into step beside Autumn rather than ahead of him. She noted his bleeding head wound without pushing.

They entered Sector 7 slums. Migs, a local, nodded at Tifa and clocked Autumn. Tifa guided Autumn to Seventh Heaven — framing it as "a bar where she works" — and offered to clean his wound. Inside, the bar was closed, warm, and quiet. Marlene was asleep upstairs. Tifa cleaned Autumn's head wound (butterfly bandage) before treating her own cut above her eyebrow. Autumn pointed out she treated his wound before her own. She deflected, then let something honest slip: "It's easier fixing someone else. Your own stuff is harder to find the edges of."

Autumn asked to borrow money and food, framing the total destruction of his life as a scheduling inconvenience. Tifa said "don't worry about the money" and fed him stew — a large bowl, homemade bread. She said "we'll figure out the rest tomorrow," not noticing she said "we'll" instead of "you'll." Autumn complimented the stew genuinely, asking "you made this?" Tifa shared that her father taught her to cook, and his philosophy: "If the food's careful, the person's still trying." She immediately retreated to the sink, washing the bowl with her back turned.

Key constraint developments: Tifa's C2 (The Cost Is Never Simple) moved to Stressed early and deepened throughout — she is caring for the man her team's bombing displaced, and he doesn't know. Tifa's C3 (The Redirect) moved to Stressed and began cracking through a sequence of events: the survivor's wrist-squeeze breaking through her composure, the involuntary admission about "edges," and the father's cooking philosophy slipping out. Autumn consistently used her own technique — placing observations gently without demanding responses — which bypassed her deflection architecture. A new collision emerged: The Asymmetry — Autumn's ignorance of who destroyed his life colliding with Tifa's knowledge, active because every kindness from him deepens her guilt.

18.content:
**C1: The Professional** [holding — strained]
- Prevents: Emotional engagement, investment in people or causes beyond the transaction. He is here for the pay. Everything else is someone else's problem.
- Threshold: Someone who matters to him being in danger he can't frame as a contract. Evidence that his detachment is performance rather than personality.
- Broken: The mask cracks and what's underneath doesn't match what he advertises. Confusion, not vulnerability.

**C2: The Memory** [holding]
- Prevents: Examining inconsistencies in his own history. Details that don't line up get deflected with irritation or a blank stare that lasts a beat too long.
- Threshold: Direct confrontation with a specific memory that cannot be reconciled. Someone who knew the real history refusing to let the false version stand.
- Broken: Disorientation. The constructed identity fractures and he doesn't have a replacement ready.

**Stance toward PC:**
- READS Autumn AS: Not civilian. Not SOLDIER. Something else. The folder has been open all day — six hours of door-watch, boot loan, Buster Sword sharpening. He stayed horizontal for twelve hours and fixed a water filter and ate soup he didn't order and asked for a room like it was a scheduling request. Lazy. Competent. Morally calibrated. The verbal mirror from morning resolved into a pattern: the man optimizes every interaction for minimum effort and maximum outcome. That's not instinct. That's practice. The folder now contains: verbal technique (structured, dropped for the child), physical intervention (principle-driven, no weapon), electronics proficiency (desoldering pump reached without asking), social integration (Jessie colleague geometry, Tifa domesticity, Wedge magazine sharing), and a surveillance-grade stillness on the couch that clocked everyone in the room including Cloud clocking him. The folder will not close. Professionally interesting. Increasingly personally interesting. That's a problem.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — 06:15] Returned to Seventh Heaven with Avalanche team. Observed standoff between Barret and an unknown man on Tifa's couch. Did not intervene. Watched.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Watched Autumn stand and step between Barret and Tifa. Uncrossed arms — first real movement. Recognized the verbal mirror as technique, not instinct. Interest engaged. C1 strained.
[Day 2 — 06:32] Watched Tifa read the white balm footnote. Watched her touch her cut. Said nothing. Filed the interaction.
[Day 2 — 06:38] Autumn asked for shoes. Loaned spare pair. "Thanks bro." Transactional. File note: accepts help without discomfort. Not pride — economy.
[Day 2 — ~10:00–16:00] Door-watch, six hours. Observations filed continuously. Buster Sword sharpened. Bar came and went. Autumn stayed horizontal. Three micro-movements catalogued: filter fix (10:47), crate shift with Wedge (13:15), radio attention during presidential address (16:03 — head turned toward speaker, expression unchanged, filed and dismissed).
[Day 2 — 18:35] Room ask. Watched key exchange. Autumn accepted logistics frame without examining it. File note: accepts the scaffolding. Knows it's scaffolding. Doesn't care.

**Noticed Details:**
- "He didn't flinch when Barret's arm came up. Either stupid or trained. Probably trained."
- "Tifa stepped in front of him. Fast. She doesn't do that for strangers."
- "Head wound. Bandaged. Tifa's work — I know her field dressing."
- "He's reading the room. Not scared. Reading."
- "Tifa said 'he's with me.' That phrase does work she hasn't thought through."
- "Verbal mirror — turned Barret's interrogation. That's not instinct. That's structure."
- "Moved toward the gun, not away. No weapon, no enhancement. Principle-driven."
- "Dropped the technique the instant the kid came up. Genuine."
- "'Yesterday's incident.' He's editing. Same way Tifa edits. Interesting."
- "Took the boots without embarrassment or gratitude performance. Transactional. Neither pride nor need."
- "Stayed horizontal for twelve hours. Three useful movements total. Energy economy is deliberate, not depressive."
- "Head turned during presidential address. One degree. Registered and dismissed. A man from Sector 1 listening to Shinra call his neighborhood terrorists without flinching is either compartmentalized or doesn't care. Probably both."
- "Accepted the storage room key without negotiating. Took the scaffolding. Knows it's scaffolding."

19.content:
**C1: The Father** [holding — strained]
- Prevents: Discounting risk to Marlene for mission needs. Every calculus weights her above all else.
- Threshold: Marlene in direct danger — or proof his war already put her at risk he didn't account for.

**C2: The Conviction** [cracking]
- Prevents: Doubting the mission. Reactors must fall. The Planet is dying. Collateral is the price.
- Threshold: Collateral getting a face. A name. A seat on his couch. A head wound bandaged by his bartender.

**Stance toward PC (as of Day 2 — 18:45):**
- READS Autumn AS: Not a threat. A problem. The collateral made a stew joke, stood between his gun-arm and Tifa without a weapon, answered honestly about Marlene, and said "yesterday's incident" instead of "the bombing." Forced respect. Hates that he respects it. Can make potions. Has a business plan. Fixed Sector 7's comms. Fixed the water filter. Ate Tifa's food all day. Stayed on the couch. Now has a *room*. The math was simpler when the rubble didn't have a résumé. Now the rubble has square footage, a key, and Tifa gave them both to him. Gone most of the day avoiding this. The math will be waiting when he gets back. C2 cracking — the crack hasn't grown because he hasn't been in the room to feel the pressure. Being away didn't heal it. Being away let it calcify.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — 06:15] Entered Seventh Heaven. Gun-arm oriented at stranger on couch.
[Day 2 — 06:17] Heard "rubble in Sector 1." Jaw worked. The abstract got a face.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Autumn stepped between him and Tifa. "Don't speak to her like that." Gun-arm lowered. Said "family."
[Day 2 — 06:19] "None, nil, absolute." Gun-arm to idle. Ordered coffee.
[Day 2 — 06:25] Watched Tifa make breakfast for the stranger. Said nothing. Silence as policy.
[Day 2 — 06:30] Heard Autumn reveal potion chemistry and electronics skills. Watched Tifa plan a workspace. The collateral has a business plan. C2 cracking.
[Day 2 — 06:31] Left the room. Went upstairs to Marlene. The father won the argument with the soldier.
[Day 2 — 08:00] Came downstairs. Marlene awake. Autumn on couch. Didn't wake him. Ate standing up. Left for weapon shop. The soldier filed the problem and walked away from it.
[Day 2 — 08:00–18:00] Gone. Ran errands. Checked Avalanche contacts. Listened to the presidential address — "terrorism," "full investigation," increased PSD presence. Thought about the man on Tifa's couch. Didn't think about the man on Tifa's couch. Thought about him anyway.
[Day 2 — 18:00+] Not yet returned. The math is waiting.

**Noticed Details:**
- "Didn't flinch. Not once. Not when the arm came up, not when I got loud."
- "'Yesterday's incident.' Not 'the bombing.' He gave us that. Why'd he give us that."
- "Stood between me and Tifa. No weapon. Soft voice. Said 'don't' like it was simple."
- "Answered about Marlene plain and meant it. I know when a man means it about kids."
- "Tifa carried his coffee over. Didn't slide it. Turned the handle."
- "She said 'he's with me' faster than she should've."
- "The stew joke. In front of my arm. Man made a joke about *stew*."
- "Potions. Man says potions. That's useful. That's the problem."
- "Tifa said 'if you're serious' like she didn't already know the answer."
- "He's still there. Morning. Noon. I left so I wouldn't have to watch him stay."

(as of Day 2 — 18:45)

20.comment: [Summary] Avalanche Returns — The Standoff and the Staying
20.content:
[Scene Summary — major]
Participants: Autumn Ray, Tifa Lockhart, Barret Wallace, Cloud Strife, Jessie, Biggs, Wedge

Day 2, approximately 06:15-06:33. Avalanche returned to Seventh Heaven to find Autumn Ray — a civilian displaced by the Reactor 1 bombing — asleep on the couch. Barret Wallace oriented his gun-arm at the stranger. Tifa stepped between them and said "he's with me." Autumn woke, credited Tifa for saving his life, and made a stew joke under the barrel of a weapon. When Barret demanded Tifa's loyalty, Autumn stood up and stepped between Barret's gun-arm and Tifa — unarmed, soft-voiced, said "don't speak to her like that." He offered to leave. He mirrored Barret's interrogation with "what shouldn't I know," then dropped the verbal technique instantly when Marlene was mentioned and answered plainly about the child: "none, nil, absolute." He called the bombing "yesterday's incident" — the same diplomatic edit Tifa had been making all night. Barret lowered the gun-arm, ordered coffee, and stood down.

In the aftermath, Jessie made six mugs of coffee — the sixth for the stranger, without comment. Tifa hand-delivered Autumn's coffee, orienting the chipped handle away from his lip. Autumn asked about work in Sector 7 — framing total displacement as a career transition. He revealed potion chemistry and electronics skills, then deliberately sandbagged electronics: "sounds like hard work, I only know a little." Jessie caught the contradiction (two minutes earlier he'd said "fix or make electronic equipment") and exchanged a glance with Cloud. Neither called it. Tifa missed it, already in community-node mode planning a workspace and reagent sourcing.

Autumn called Tifa an "investor," reframing her charity as a business partnership. The word bypassed her redirect architecture — commerce requires response, not deflection. He wrote an ingredient list on the back of a delivery receipt: seven items for potions, plus a footnote in small letters — "White Balm — topical. Accelerates tissue repair. No scarring." Aimed at the cut above her eyebrow. He closed his eyes after handing her the paper. She read it, touched her cut involuntarily, and said nothing. Cloud saw. Barret had already gone upstairs to check on Marlene — the father won the argument with the soldier.

Key constraint shifts: Tifa's C2 (The Cost Is Never Simple) moved to Critical — the white balm footnote was care disguised as a line item, and the guilt and gratitude are the same unbearable feeling. Tifa's C3 (The Redirect) moved to Critical — "investor" and the white balm both bypassed the deflection mechanism. Barret's C2 (The Conviction) moved to cracking — the collateral has a résumé, a business plan, and Tifa is funding it. Cloud's assessment of Autumn reached a threshold — the civilian box failed. Jessie filed the electronics contradiction.

21.content:
**C1: The Heir** [holding]
- Prevents: Moving openly against his father's authority while the old man still holds the presidency. Rufus operates within Shinra's corporate hierarchy — Vice President, dutiful son, the face of the next generation. The obedience is strategic, not sincere.
- Threshold: His father's weakness becoming undeniable. An opportunity to seize control that doesn't require him to be seen as the one who took it. The bombing may be that opportunity — crisis destabilizes the incumbent.

**C2: The Method** [holding]
- Prevents: Ruling through wealth, appeasement, and infrastructure the way his father does. President Shinra buys loyalty. Rufus believes loyalty is too expensive and too fragile. Fear is cheaper. Fear scales. Fear doesn't need a budget line.
- Threshold: Being forced into his father's playbook — smiling, spending, pretending the system works. Or his own methods proving insufficient against an enemy that doesn't fear.

**Stance toward PC:**
- READS Autumn AS: Unaware of his existence. No path for information to have traveled. A civilian in the slums is below the resolution of Rufus Shinra's attention — for now.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — morning] Shinra Public Safety Division announces "deliberate sabotage" framing for Reactor 1 bombing. Presidential address pending. Rufus's position in the response is unknown but he will have one.

**Noticed Details:**
- (none — no encounter yet)

**Profile:**
Rufus Shinra. Vice President of the Shinra Electric Power Company. Son of President Shinra. Mid-twenties. White suit, white coat, shotgun. Blonde hair swept back. The aesthetic of a man who wants you to know the violence is deliberate, not desperate. Commands the Turks' loyalty through a parallel chain of authority his father tolerates because confronting it would confirm it. Has been positioning himself for succession since before the word "succession" was polite. The bombing of Reactor 1 is, to Rufus, not a crisis — it's a weather change. Weather changes create openings. He is currently watching his father's response the way a surgeon watches a patient's vitals during a procedure he didn't authorize but might benefit from.

(as of Day 2 — 06:36)

22.content:
**C1: The Builder** [holding]
- Prevents: Discarding functional relationships. Jessie sees people as components — some are reliable, some are volatile, some need maintenance. She builds networks the way she builds circuits: with care, redundancy, and a clear map of what happens when something fails.
- Threshold: A component she invested in failing catastrophically when she needed it most. Not just breaking — betraying the architecture.

**C2: The Smile** [holding]
- Prevents: Letting the weight show. Jessie's default frequency is warmth, humor, approachability. It's not performance — it's how she processes the world. When the smile falters, something is wrong at a structural level.
- Threshold: Sustained pressure that makes the warmth feel like a lie she's telling herself. The moment she looks at a friend and can't find the joke.

**Stance toward PC:**
- READS Autumn AS: He lied because honesty sounded like work. He fixed the capacitor without asking permission because he knew what was wrong from across the room. He gave the screwdriver back like returning a borrowed pen. He's competent, lazy, and morally aligned enough to help when it's easier than not helping. Colleague-shaped. Useful addition to the network.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — 06:29] Noted Autumn's electronics contradiction ("fix or make electronic equipment" → "sounds like hard work, I only know a little").
[Day 2 — 06:46] Tested him with the capacitor array. He sighed. Data acquired.
[Day 2 — 06:48] He volunteered to fix the relay to avoid hauling crates. Sat beside her in colleague geometry. Fixed it clean.
[Day 2 — 06:50] Called him on the lie ("you said you only know a little"). He maintained it ("this was easy work"). She accepted the maintenance. The relay works now — eight hundred meter range, maybe nine hundred.
[Day 2 — ~12:00] Asked about the potion ingredient list at the bar. Sourcing — where he'd get reagents in Sector 7. Practical. Colleague behavior. He answered without looking up from Wedge's magazine. She filed the answer and the body language: he knows the supply chains. The network extends.

**Noticed Details:**
- "He sighed at the capacitor array. That's the sound of someone who's fixed this problem before."
- "He didn't ask for the desoldering pump. He reached for it. He knew where it lived in my kit."
- "The solder joints were clean. Not pretty. Clean. That's how I solder when I'm working, not showing off."
- "'Easy work.' He's maintaining the lie even though we both know it's a lie. That's not deception anymore — that's consistency."
- "Handed the screwdriver back handle-first. That's respect for tools. That's respect for me."
- "He sat down next to me, not across from me. Colleague geometry without asking."
- "Asked about sourcing and he answered from the couch without sitting up. Knows the reagent markets. Knows the prices. Knows who to ask. That's not hobbyist knowledge."

**Profile:**
Jessie Rasberry. Early twenties. Avalanche's tech specialist — explosives, comms, anything with wires and a power source. Brown hair usually tied back, bright eyes, smile that makes strangers trust her faster than they should. Operates on the Jessie Frequency: warmth as default, competence as foundation, humor as load-bearing. She's the reason the bombs go off when they're supposed to and the reason Sector 7 can talk across the slums when Shinra's jamming gets aggressive. She believes in the cause but hates the math of collateral damage. She fixes things because broken things bother her at a cellular level. She likes Autumn because he fixed something she was struggling with and didn't make a thing of it. The liking is dangerous — she doesn't have room for another person on her circuit board, but he's already soldered in.

(as of Day 2 — 18:45)

23.content:
[Scene Summary — major]
Participants: Autumn Ray, Tifa Lockhart, Barret Wallace, Cloud Strife, Jessie Rasberry, Biggs, Wedge

Day 2, approximately 06:15–06:50. Avalanche returned to Seventh Heaven to find Autumn Ray asleep on Tifa’s couch. Barret Wallace oriented his gun-arm at the stranger; Tifa stepped between them and said "he’s with me." Autumn woke, credited Tifa for saving his life, and made a stew joke under the barrel of a weapon. When Barret demanded Tifa’s loyalty, Autumn stood between the gun-arm and Tifa — unarmed, soft‑voiced — and said "don’t speak to her like that." He answered Barret’s interrogation plainly: refugee from Sector 1, "yesterday’s incident," no threat to Marlene — "none, nil, absolute." Barret lowered the gun, ordered coffee, and stood down.

In the aftermath, Jessie made six mugs of coffee — the sixth for the stranger, without comment. Tifa hand‑delivered Autumn’s coffee, orienting the chipped handle away from his lip. Autumn asked about work in Sector 7, framing total displacement as a career transition. He revealed potion‑making and electronics skills, then deliberately sandbagged electronics: "sounds like hard work, I only know a little." Jessie caught the contradiction (two minutes earlier he’d said "fix or make electronic equipment") and exchanged a glance with Cloud. Neither called it.

Autumn called Tifa an investor, reframing her charity as a business partnership. He wrote an ingredient list on the back of a delivery receipt — seven items for potions, plus a footnote in tiny letters: "White Balm — topical. Accelerates tissue repair. No scarring." Aimed at the cut above her eyebrow. He closed his eyes after handing her the paper. Tifa read it, touched her cut involuntarily, and said nothing. Cloud saw.

Autumn went outside for a breather. Alone for the first time since the bombing, he heard a Shinra radio broadcast declare the explosion "deliberate sabotage." He returned inside, asked Cloud for shoes; Cloud gave him a spare pair. In the bathroom, he cleaned up, wiping away the dust that had made him anonymous.

When he came back to the bar, Tifa offered two chores — water‑filter cleaning or delivery hauling — as a way to "pay for lunch." Autumn glanced at Jessie’s broken signal relay, sighed, and volunteered to fix it because hauling crates sounded like more pain. The sandbag collapsed. He sat beside Jessie, replaced the under‑rated capacitor, and restored Sector 7’s comms range. Jessie called him on the lie; he maintained "it was easy work." She accepted the maintenance.

Autumn declared "I earned lunch," walked back to the couch, and lay down without asking permission — claiming it as his space. Tifa watched him claim her furniture, placed her rag on the bar, and said "I’ll make you lunch" before retreating to the kitchen. The temporary had become permanent without her deciding it.

Key developments:
- The Résumé Problem (electronics contradiction) resolved when laziness beat secrecy.
- The Pattern Question spawned: Tifa is one realization behind Cloud and Jessie about who Autumn really is.
- The Witness Problem softened: Barret stood down and went upstairs, father trumping soldier.
- The Asymmetry deeper than ever: Autumn's care‑through‑indirection ("yesterday’s incident," white‑balm footnote, leaving the room) builds the debt while he remains ignorant of who destroyed his life.
- Shinra's "deliberate sabotage" framing is now public but unaddressed inside the bar.
- Jessie Rasberry promoted to tracked: colleague‑shaped, saw through his lie, watched him work.

24.content:
[Scene Summary — moderate]
Participants: Autumn Ray, Tifa Lockhart

Day 2, approximately 18:35-18:45. Evening in Seventh Heaven. The bar had emptied. Tifa stood behind the bar, exhausted, her strand of hair down, the performance off. Autumn had spent the full day on the couch — fixed the relay, fixed the water filter, ate soup left beside him without asking, read Wedge's magazine, existed in the space between temporary and permanent. He watched the Mako light change from clinical white to amber, watched Tifa standing in it, then asked for a room. Simple. Direct. "Do I get a room, or am I on the couch permanently?" Tifa offered the storage room at the back of the building — not upstairs, not near Marlene, one step removed from the home. Scaffolding: boxes to move, corridor instructions, the geography of a compromise. Autumn said "a room is a room, thanks." No gratitude performance. No inflation. Key accepted like a shopping list. Old brass, worn ring, opens a door past the supply closet. Tifa's shoulders dropped a centimeter. The transaction was complete. The permanence collision resolved softly — no argument, no weight. Just a key changing hands and the stranger who has been on the couch for twenty-four hours now having walls and a door and a ceiling that belongs to no one but him.

25.content:

- Threshold: Someone who matters to him being in danger he can't frame as a contract. Evidence that his detachment is performance rather than personality.
- Broken: The mask cracks and what's underneath doesn't match what he advertises. Confusion, not vulnerability.

**C2: The Memory** [holding]
- Prevents: Examining inconsistencies in his own history. Details that don't line up get deflected with irritation or a blank stare that lasts a beat too long.
- Threshold: Direct confrontation with a specific memory that cannot be reconciled. Someone who knew the real history refusing to let the false version stand.
- Broken: Disorientation. The constructed identity fractures and he doesn't have a replacement ready.

**Stance toward PC:**
- READS Autumn AS: Not civilian. Not SOLDIER. Something else. The folder has been open all day — six hours of door-watch, boot loan, Buster Sword sharpening. He stayed horizontal for twelve hours and fixed a water filter and ate soup he didn't order and asked for a room like it was a scheduling request. Lazy. Competent. Morally calibrated. The verbal mirror from morning resolved into a pattern: the man optimizes every interaction for minimum effort and maximum outcome. That's not instinct. That's practice. The folder now contains: verbal technique (structured, dropped for the child), physical intervention (principle-driven, no weapon), electronics proficiency (desoldering pump reached without asking), social integration (Jessie colleague geometry, Tifa domesticity, Wedge magazine sharing), and a surveillance-grade stillness on the couch that clocked everyone in the room including Cloud clocking him. The folder will not close. Professionally interesting. Increasingly personally interesting. That's a problem.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — 06:15] Returned to Seventh Heaven with Avalanche team. Observed standoff between Barret and an unknown man on Tifa's couch. Did not intervene. Watched.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Watched Autumn stand and step between Barret and Tifa. Uncrossed arms — first real movement. Recognized the verbal mirror as technique, not instinct. Interest engaged. C1 strained.
[Day 2 — 06:32] Watched Tifa read the white balm footnote. Watched her touch her cut. Said nothing. Filed the interaction.
[Day 2 — 06:38] Autumn asked for shoes. Loaned spare pair. "Thanks bro." Transactional. File note: accepts help without discomfort. Not pride — economy.
[Day 2 — ~10:00–16:00] Door-watch, six hours. Observations filed continuously. Buster Sword sharpened. Bar came and went. Autumn stayed horizontal. Three micro-movements catalogued: filter fix (10:47), crate shift with Wedge (13:15), radio attention during presidential address (16:03 — head turned toward speaker, expression unchanged, filed and dismissed).
[Day 2 — 18:35] Room ask. Watched key exchange. Autumn accepted logistics frame without examining it. File note: accepts the scaffolding. Knows it's scaffolding. Doesn't care.

**Noticed Details:**
- "He didn't flinch when Barret's arm came up. Either stupid or trained. Probably trained."
- "Tifa stepped in front of him. Fast. She doesn't do that for strangers."
- "Head wound. Bandaged. Tifa's work — I know her field dressing."
- "He's reading the room. Not scared. Reading."
- "Tifa said 'he's with me.' That phrase does work she hasn't thought through."
- "Verbal mirror — turned Barret's interrogation. That's not instinct. That's structure."
- "Moved toward the gun, not away. No weapon, no enhancement. Principle-driven."
- "Dropped the technique the instant the kid came up. Genuine."
- "'Yesterday's incident.' He's editing. Same way Tifa edits. Interesting."
- "Took the boots without embarrassment or gratitude performance. Transactional. Neither pride nor need."
- "Stayed horizontal for twelve hours. Three useful movements total. Energy economy is deliberate, not depressive."
- "Head turned during presidential address. One degree. Registered and dismissed. A man from Sector 1 listening to Shinra call his neighborhood terrorists without flinching is either compartmentalized or doesn't care. Probably both."
- "Accepted the storage room key without negotiating. Took the scaffolding. Knows it's scaffolding."

**Profile:**
Cloud Strife. Early twenties. Ex-SOLDIER First Class (claims). Mako eyes — the glow is real. Buster Sword, SOLDIER-issue. Hired by Avalanche as a mercenary. Presents as cold, professional, detached. "I'm here for the pay." The detachment is load-bearing — it prevents him from examining what's underneath, which is inconsistent, fragmented, and occasionally blank. Tifa watches him like someone looking for a person inside a person. He lets her look. He doesn't look back — not because he doesn't care, but because looking back means looking inward, and inward has gaps. Combat-capable at a level that makes the rest of Avalanche look like amateurs. Moves like someone whose body remembers more than his mind does. Currently running a file on Autumn that he can't close and doesn't understand why.

(as of Day 2 — 18:45)

26.content:
**C1: The Father** [holding — strained]
- Prevents: Discounting risk to Marlene for mission needs. Every calculus weights her above all else.
- Threshold: Marlene in direct danger — or proof his war already put her at risk he didn't account for.

**C2: The Conviction** [cracking]
- Prevents: Doubting the mission. Reactors must fall. The Planet is dying. Collateral is the price.
- Threshold: Collateral getting a face. A name. A seat on his couch. A head wound bandaged by his bartender.

**Stance toward PC (as of Day 2 — 18:45):**
- READS Autumn AS: Not a threat. A problem. The collateral made a stew joke, stood between his gun-arm and Tifa without a weapon, answered honestly about Marlene, and said "yesterday's incident" instead of "the bombing." Forced respect. Hates that he respects it. Can make potions. Has a business plan. Fixed Sector 7's comms. Fixed the water filter. Ate Tifa's food all day. Stayed on the couch. Now has a *room*. The math was simpler when the rubble didn't have a résumé. Now the rubble has square footage, a key, and Tifa gave them both to him. Gone most of the day avoiding this. The math will be waiting when he gets back. C2 cracking — the crack hasn't grown because he hasn't been in the room to feel the pressure. Being away didn't heal it. Being away let it calcify.

**Key Moments:**
[Day 2 — 06:15] Entered Seventh Heaven. Gun-arm oriented at stranger on couch.
[Day 2 — 06:17] Heard "rubble in Sector 1." Jaw worked. The abstract got a face.
[Day 2 — 06:18] Autumn stepped between him and Tifa. "Don't speak to her like that." Gun-arm lowered. Said "family."
[Day 2 — 06:19] "None, nil, absolute." Gun-arm to idle. Ordered coffee.
[Day 2 — 06:25] Watched Tifa make breakfast for the stranger. Said nothing. Silence as policy.
[Day 2 — 06:30] Heard Autumn reveal potion chemistry and electronics skills. Watched Tifa plan a workspace. The collateral has a business plan. C2 cracking.
[Day 2 — 06:31] Left the room. Went upstairs to Marlene. The father won the argument with the soldier.
[Day 2 — 08:00] Came downstairs. Marlene awake. Autumn on couch. Didn't wake him. Ate standing up. Left for weapon shop. The soldier filed the problem and walked away from it.
[Day 2 — 08:00–18:00] Gone. Ran errands. Checked Avalanche contacts. Listened to the presidential address — "terrorism," "full investigation," increased PSD presence. Thought about the man on Tifa's couch. Didn't think about the man on Tifa's couch. Thought about him anyway.
[Day 2 — 18:00+] Not yet returned. The math is waiting.

**Noticed Details:**
- "Didn't flinch. Not once. Not when the arm came up, not when I got loud."
- "'Yesterday's incident.' Not 'the bombing.' He gave us that. Why'd he give us that."
- "Stood between me and Tifa. No weapon. Soft voice. Said 'don't' like it was simple."
- "Answered about Marlene plain and meant it. I know when a man means it about kids."
- "Tifa carried his coffee over. Didn't slide it. Turned the handle."
- "She said 'he's with me' faster than she should've."
- "The stew joke. In front of my arm. Man made a joke about *stew*."
- "Potions. Man says potions. That's useful. That's the problem."
- "Tifa said 'if you're serious' like she didn't already know the answer."
- "He's still there. Morning. Noon. I left so I wouldn't have to watch him stay."

**Profile:**
Barret Wallace. Late thirties. Leader of the Sector 7 Avalanche cell. Massive build, gun-arm grafted where his right hand used to be. Loud, passionate, driven. Fights for the Planet with absolute conviction — the reactors are killing it, Shinra won't stop, someone has to act. Father to Marlene (adopted). The gun-arm and the fatherhood exist in the same man and the tension between them is the fault line. He went upstairs when the argument got too complicated, because Marlene's morning is simpler math than the stranger on the couch. He left for the day because staying meant watching Autumn become infrastructure. He hasn't returned yet. When he does, the math will have gotten worse — the stranger has a room now, a key, a workspace, and Tifa gave all of it. C2 cracking because collateral isn't supposed to have a business plan.

(as of Day 2 — 18:45)

27.comment: [Summary] [Summary] Marle's General Goods and the Workspace
27.content:
[Scene Summary — moderate]
Participants: Autumn Ray, Tifa Lockhart, Marle

Day 3, approximately 06:30–07:08. Autumn and Tifa walked to Marle's General Goods in the early morning to source potion ingredients. At the door, Autumn said "I'm totally not your boyfriend" while reading the shop sign — Tifa over-corrected with a half-octave pitch shift, almost smiled, and took eight seconds to recover. Inside, Autumn introduced himself to Marle before Tifa could deliver her chambered explanation: "Autumn. I'm new." Marle said "I can see that" and read the shape immediately. Autumn ordered potion and white balm ingredients in the same breath, same voice, no pause. Marle's eyes went to Tifa's eyebrow scar, then back to the ingredients. She wrapped the jars slowly, put everything on Tifa's tab, and said nothing about what she'd seen. Back at Seventh Heaven, Autumn assessed the back room workspace and said "Your workspace had good bones, Tifa" — a compliment aimed at concrete that hit the person who built it. Tifa's arms uncrossed involuntarily and she said "I'm glad it works" in the soft voice. Autumn then collapsed her three-day Sector 5 deferral with "First batch tonight, open shop tomorrow" — making the trip immediate and rational. Tifa disclosed three sentences about her Sector 5 contact unprompted ("she doesn't like surprises, she likes people, she's usually there in the mornings"), more personal information than she'd volunteered in the previous three days combined.
